danger_signals.visual_form: Humanoid enemies with police-like armor bearing LARD insignia, corrupted law enforcement aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Alien corruption of Earth authorities; hostile human-alien hybrids; civilization infrastructure turned against player

danger_signals.visual_form: Mutated and distinctly non-human enemy forms throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Direct alien invasion threat; varying threat levels based on creature variety

danger_signals.visual_form: Damaged buildings, collapsed structures, debris scattered in Hollywood and urban environments
danger_signals.meaning: Invasion has already caused widespread devastation; world-state shows active conflict zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark areas requiring night vision goggles to navigate safely
danger_signals.meaning: Danger present but obscured; enemies may be concealed; player vulnerability increased

safety_signals.visual_form: Toggleable light sources in environments that illuminate dark spaces when activated
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency over visibility; illumination reveals safe paths and hidden threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Medkits available throughout levels for player collection
safety_signals.meaning: Survival resources present; designer-intended recovery opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Jetpack, protective boots, aqua-lung, HoloDuke device as pickable items
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards are traversable with proper tools; game provides means to overcome obstacles

safety_signals.visual_form: Air ducts, back doors, sewers, multiple pathways visible in architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Player has tactical options; can avoid direct confrontation; environmental navigation offers safety alternatives

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Duke Nukem character (protagonist); associated with Hollywood setting
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hollywood locations, urban Earth environments, human-origin architecture and props

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Various mutated creature designs; distinct non-human morphology
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple alien creature types with varying appearances and threat levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence of aliens indicates invaded territory; corruption of human spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: LARD insignia on uniforms; police-derivative armor and weapons
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Police-based humanoid enemies with alien hybridization visible in design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Law enforcement areas now occupied by Pig Cops; corruption of institutional spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destroyed buildings, collapsed structures, environmental damage throughout Hollywood and urban areas; presence of multiple alien creature types; human-alien hybrids (Pig Cops) patrolling previously secure locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively pursue player; hostile encounters unavoidable in many areas; world-state communicated through pervasive destruction and constant threat presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Broken fire hydrants, malfunctioning systems, interactive environmental damage; light switches that control visibility; non-functional safety systems requiring player intervention
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manually restore basic functions (activate lights); world no longer supports human habitation without player agency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Areas with medkits, protective equipment, and peaceful interactive elements (fountains, tipping strippers providing distraction moments)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Brief respite from combat; environmental storytelling suggests remnants of normal civilian life persisting despite invasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hot terrain, toxic areas, underwater sections, vertical inaccessible spaces without equipment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must locate and equip protective items (boots, aqua-lung, jetpack) to traverse; world-state communicated through environmental barriers rather than enemies

environmental_storytelling.example: Player can destroy most props and environmental objects, revealing interactive world and agency over destruction narrative
Hollywood locations and urban environments show visible damage, collapsed structures, and evidence of conflict without explicit cinematics
LAPD transformed into Pig Cops with LARD insignia; visual symbol of authority structure compromised by alien invasion
Presence of strippers and interactive civilian elements (fountains, light switches) suggests normalcy disrupted but not completely erased
Placement of medkits, protective equipment, and weapons throughout levels tells story of human survival efforts and pre-positioned defense preparations
Air ducts, back doors, and sewers suggest evacuation routes and hidden resistance infrastructure within invaded cities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile engagement with player; organized law enforcement-style opposition indicating systematic alien infiltration of human authority
Varied threat responses; multiple creature types suggest coordinated invasion force with specialized units
Active combat engagement; environmental interaction; survival through resource gathering and tactical navigation
Player-triggered responses (light activation, prop destruction); communication of player agency and world malleability

notes: Duke Nukem 3D communicates world-state primarily through environmental degradation, enemy type presence, and architectural corruption. The civilization's infrastructure (police, lighting, buildings) has been visibly compromised by alien invasion. Danger signals are manifested through enemy encounters and environmental hazards, while safety is communicated through resource availability and level design offering tactical alternatives. The faction system is implicit rather than explicit: human vs. alien vs. corrupted-human (Pig Cops). Visual communication relies heavily on environmental storytelling rather than UI elements, consistent with 1996 FPS design paradigms. The game communicates political situation (Earth under invasion, authority structures compromised) through visual manifestation of corruption and destruction rather than exposition.
